mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns when UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) create uncertainty about future rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS has a coefficient < 0.05 in OLS regression, or 20d returns are not significantly lower when both series rise.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS, increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by 0.5% in 3 months, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS in a regression is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing market activity (HOUST) reflects robust demand conditions that support corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances with rising HOUST, or the correlation between HOUST and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side momentum that boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances with rising INDPRO, or the coefficient on INDPRO in a regression is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals insufficient job creation to sustain consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns when PAYEMS falls below 130,000.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 130,000, or the correlation between PAYEMS and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong demand conditions that drive corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS has a coefficient < 0.05 in OLS regression, or 20d returns are not significantly higher when both series rise.
